In rerun::external::re_memory

?

Struct rerun::external::re_memory::MemoryLimit

source · 
pub struct MemoryLimit {
    pub limit: Option<i64>,
}

Fields§

§limit: Option<i64>

Limit in bytes.

This is primarily compared to what is reported by crate::AccountingAllocator (‘counted’). We limit based on this instead of resident (RSS) because counted is what we have immediate control over, while RSS depends on what our allocator (MiMalloc) decides to do.

Implementations§

source§

impl MemoryLimit

source

pub fn parse(limit: &str) -> Result<MemoryLimit, String>

The limit can either be absolute (e.g. “16GB”) or relative (e.g. “50%”).

source

pub fn is_exceeded_by(&self, mem_use: &MemoryUse) -> Option<f32>

Returns how large fraction of memory we should free to go down to the exact limit.
